exercises.name: Metronome
exercises.rule: Steady tempo. No racing, no dragging.
exercises.why: Builds an internal clock — listeners trust steady speakers more.

prompts.text: Walk through how your team plans a sprint.
Describe your morning routine from alarm to first meeting.
Walk me through your weekend.
Explain how you'd onboard a new hire over their first two weeks.
Talk through how a mortgage works for a first-time buyer.
Describe the architecture of the last system you shipped.
Walk me through your quarterly business review from intro to close.
Explain how an MRI scan actually produces an image.
Take me through how you graded your last batch of student essays.
Describe the play-by-play of the last close game you watched.
Walk through your incident response process end to end.
Explain how a bill becomes law.
Talk through the plot of a film you saw recently.
Walk a junior PM through your weekly planning cadence.
Explain how your team makes a hiring decision from screen to offer.
Take me through a layoff conversation you'd have to deliver tomorrow.
Walk me through the most complicated deal you've closed.

exercises.name: Strategic Pause
exercises.rule: One deliberate pause before each new beat.
exercises.why: Silence is signal — it tells listeners the next thing matters.

prompts.text: Pitch our product to a board member you just met.
Make the case for hiring two more engineers this quarter.
Open a sales call with a CFO who has five minutes.
Introduce yourself at a panel before answering the first question.
Pitch a friend on the book you couldn't put down.
Recommend a treatment plan to a patient who looks scared.
Brief the executive team on a missed quarter.
Open a town hall after a big company change.
Pitch a new course design to your department head.
Make the case for switching cloud providers.
Sell a skeptical investor on your fund's thesis.
Open a wedding toast for someone you've known fifteen years.
Pitch your team on a new design direction they didn't ask for.
Open a postmortem to a room that wants to assign blame.
Introduce your startup at a demo day in 60 seconds.
Pitch a coach on a tactical change before a championship game.

exercises.name: Silence Over Filler
exercises.rule: Every um or uh replaced with silence.
exercises.why: Trains comfort with the gap. Silence reads as confidence.

prompts.text: Tell me about a time you were wrong.
Explain a decision you regret making at work.
Describe the worst feedback you've ever received.
Walk through a bug you caused in production.
Tell me about a deal you lost and why.
Explain a diagnosis you initially missed.
Describe a time you had to fire someone.
Talk about a relationship that ended badly.
Explain a topic outside your expertise — like quantum computing.
Describe what you'd change about your last performance review.
Tell me why your last project shipped late.
Explain your team's biggest blind spot.
Walk through a number you can't explain to leadership.
Describe a moment you froze in front of a class.
Talk about a game where you choked under pressure.
Explain what's most uncertain about your roadmap right now.
Describe a creative project that didn't land.

exercises.name: Two-Beat Landing
exercises.rule: Land every claim. Count two beats before moving on.
exercises.why: Gives weight to claims — rushing past them tells the listener they don't matter.

prompts.text: Make the case for our pricing change.
Argue why your team should own the new platform.
Defend the budget cut you just announced.
Make three arguments for adopting a new framework.
Convince a parent to switch their child to your school.
Argue why this drug should get FDA approval.
Defend the trade-off you made on the last release.
Make the case for hiring senior over junior this year.
Argue your startup is worth the valuation you're asking for.
Defend a marketing budget you tripled.
Pitch why your novel deserves a publisher.
Make the case for benching your star player.
Argue why a four-day workweek would work for your org.
Make three claims about what your product does best.
Convince a teammate to take on a stretch project.
Defend why you're the right person to lead this initiative.

exercises.name: Slow Cooker
exercises.rule: Deliver at 70% normal speed without losing energy.
exercises.why: Trains presence — speed often masks lack of conviction.

prompts.text: Explain how you handled the production outage.
Walk through the moment you realized the launch was failing.
Describe the ER shift where everything went wrong.
Talk through your reaction when the market dropped 8% in a day.
Explain how you broke the news of a missed target to the CEO.
Walk me through the security incident from detection to all-clear.
Describe coaching your team through a fourth-quarter collapse.
Talk through the day a student had a crisis in your class.
Explain how you responded to a hostile customer escalation.
Walk through the call where you had to deliver a difficult diagnosis.
Describe the last creative deadline you nearly missed.
Talk about racing to ship before a competitor's announcement.
Explain how you handled a sudden round of layoffs.
Walk through the day a portfolio company missed payroll.
Describe the moment a key teammate quit during crunch.
Talk through how you packed for an unexpected trip in an hour.

exercises.name: Punctuation Breathing
exercises.rule: One breath per sentence boundary. No run-ons.
exercises.why: Breath sets the punctuation. Listeners hear what you breathe.

prompts.text: Tell me about your career so far.
Walk me through how you got into this field.
Tell the story of how your company was founded.
Describe the patient case that shaped your practice.
Tell me about the longest project you've ever shipped.
Walk through your investment thesis from origin to today.
Tell the story of your favorite student or mentee.
Describe how your team came together over the last year.
Tell me about the championship season you'll never forget.
Walk through the writing process for your last big piece.
Tell me about a friendship that changed how you think.
Describe your relationship with your first manager.
Walk me through the year you almost gave up.
Tell the origin story of the product you're proudest of.
Describe the lab discovery that took five years to land.
Tell me about a city you lived in that changed you.

exercises.name: The Stretch
exercises.rule: Extend the most important word by 30%.
exercises.why: Stress through duration — the word the listener remembers.

prompts.text: What's the most important thing you learned this year?
Describe the single best hire you've ever made.
What's the riskiest bet your company has placed?
Tell me the one number that defines this quarter.
Describe the most memorable patient you've ever treated.
What's the most surprising result from your last experiment?
Tell me about the proudest moment of your coaching career.
What's the most dangerous assumption your team is making?
Describe the best lesson a student ever taught you.
What's the one feature that made your product take off?
Tell me the most important book you've ever read.
Describe the worst mistake you've watched a peer make.
What's the single biggest risk in your portfolio right now?
Tell me the most underrated skill in your industry.
Describe the most beautiful piece of code you've ever read.
What's the one thing you'd never compromise on as a leader?

exercises.name: Tempo Shift
exercises.rule: Open slow. Accelerate into the proof. Decelerate at the close.
exercises.why: Dynamic pacing keeps attention; flat tempo loses it.

prompts.text: Brief the team on next quarter's goals.
Present your design proposal to a skeptical stakeholder.
Walk leadership through your security roadmap.
Present this quarter's earnings to the board.
Brief your team on a strategy reset after a tough quarter.
Present your research findings at a conference keynote.
Open a parent-teacher night with your year's plan.
Brief the coaching staff on tomorrow's game plan.
Pitch your new product line at the all-hands.
Walk a new patient through their treatment journey.
Present a quarterly portfolio update to your LPs.
Brief a journalist on your company's biggest story this year.
Open a workshop on a topic you've taught a hundred times.
Present your team's roadmap to the engineering org.
Pitch a documentary idea to a production company.
Brief the rest of your team after a strategy off-site.

exercises.name: Beat the Buzzer
exercises.rule: Hit the 45-second target window. No more, no less.
exercises.why: Builds a wall-clock for delivery — useful for status, intros, pitches.

prompts.text: 45 seconds: introduce yourself to a new team.
45 seconds: give your weekly status update.
45 seconds: pitch your startup to an investor in an elevator.
45 seconds: summarize the production outage for executives.
45 seconds: brief a colleague before they cover for you.
45 seconds: present your patient's case to the attending.
45 seconds: open a town hall after big news.
45 seconds: pitch a movie idea to a tired producer.
45 seconds: explain your investment thesis to a new LP.
45 seconds: introduce a guest speaker to your class.
45 seconds: brief the press after a tough loss.
45 seconds: deliver a wedding toast that lands.
45 seconds: recap the quarter to your board chair.
45 seconds: pitch a feature cut to product leadership.
45 seconds: explain your research to a smart non-expert.
45 seconds: give the eulogy intro you'd want delivered for you.
